question.content: Using the transistor output characteristics, find quiescent collector-emitter voltage and maximum undistorted output RMS voltage for RL=infinity and RL=3 kOhm. Output exactly four comma-separated values in the order UCEQ_inf,Uom_inf,UCEQ_3k,Uom_3k, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text. Answer format: UCEQ_inf,Uom_inf,UCEQ_3k,Uom_3k in that order; write only the final results, with no units, explanatory text, or equals signs.

step_1.content: Draw the corresponding DC load lines for both load cases and read Q-point intersections.
step_1.key_point: Draw the corresponding DC load lines for both load cases.

step_3.content: Analyze panel 1 of 2 and record the intermediate relation needed before moving to the next panel.
step_3.key_point: Set up governing relation for quiescent collector-emitter voltage and maximum undistorted.

step_5.content: Compute Uom from available swing to the saturation boundary for each case.
step_5.key_point: Uom from available swing to the saturation boundary for each case.
